channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Green Hell Sales Hit 12m as Seventh Anniversary Looms
item.content:encoded:
Developer and publisher Creepy Jar has announced that its Amazon jungle survival sim Green Hell has hit a new sales milestone as the game's eighth anniversary looms.

As revealed earlier today via a Steam post, Green Hell has hit 12 million sales since its Early Access launch back in 2018. It's received a number of updates and additions since then, as well as getting console ports and even the ability to play in VR (which I wouldn't recommend if you're an arachnophobe).

Somewhat confusingly, Creepy Jar says Green Hell launched in Early Access in 2019, but that's actually when the full 1.0 version of the game arrived; the Early Access release happened in August 2018, so the studio had a year or so to polish and refine Green Hell before its official launch.

Creepy Jar says it's done its best since 2019 to "create an immersive experience", looking back to a time in 2016 when the studio consisted of just five developers "creating early prototypes of a survival simulator". You know how the rest of the story goes.

Today, Green Hell is still enjoying a healthy player count, with just over 9,000 people checking the game out on Steam at its 24-hour peak (at time of writing). There aren't many games that can say they're still that popular almost seven years out from release.

If you're not familiar with Green Hell, it's a survival sim that plonks you (and, optionally, your co-op partner) into the middle of the Amazon rainforest and tasks you with surviving. You know the drill: build shelters, avoid hostile wildlife, try not to die of poisoning, that sort of thing.

You can check out Green Hell right now on PC, PlayStation, Xbox, and Nintendo Switch if you want to see what all the fuss is about, and you can check out our review from back at the 1.0 launch right here.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/green-hell-sales-12m
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Tekken 8 Kunimitsu Release Date and First Gameplay Revealed
item.content:encoded:
Bandai Namco has announced the release date for Tekken 8's latest DLC character, the aptly-named kunoichi Kunimitsu, and she's gracing the game with her ninjutsu arts next month.

As revealed in a new trailer, Kunimitsu, who was first revealed as part of the game's third season back in February, will be added to Tekken 8's roster on June 1st. She joins other DLC characters like iconic assassin Anna Williams and Mishima patriarch Heihachi.

In Kunimitsu's debut trailer, she's shown to be a speedy character who relies on quick combos and ninjutsu techniques rather than raw power, so if you're expecting a Jack-style powerhouse, you're probably going to be disappointed.

As is customary when it comes to Tekken 8 character introduction trailers, Kunimitsu is let loose on a host of characters throughout her debut showcase, battling the likes of Yoshimitsu, Lidia, and Eddy Gordo, to name but a few.

It's Raven, though, who bears the brunt of Kunimitsu's wrath; he's her opponent for the majority of the trailer's second half, and it's on him she unleashes her Rage Art, which sees her delivering blow after blow with her own sword as well as a host of phantom blades.

Kunimitsu is the first character to be released as part of Tekken 8's third season of DLC, with two more, Bob and Roger Jr., slated to launch in summer and fall respectively. A fourth is also coming at some point, and we'll learn who that is during the Combo Breaker event on May 24th.

News of Kunimitsu's release date comes after a period of upheaval for Tekken and Bandai Namco. Longtime Tekken producer and face Katsuhiro Harada, who's been closely associated with the franchise for decades, announced he would leave the studio back in December.

You can check out Tekken 8 right now on PC, PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series X|S. Kunimitsu will be added to the game as DLC on June 1st, with Early Access available from May 27th.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/tekken-8-kunimitsu-release-date
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Heroes of Might and Magic: Olden Era Sales Hit 500k in 72 Hours
item.content:encoded:
Developer Unfrozen and publisher Hooded Horse, along with Ubisoft (which owns the game's IP), have announced that Heroes of Might and Magic: Olden Era has hit yet another huge sales milestone.

The strategy RPG revival, which crossed 250,000 sales in its first day, has now passed the 500,000 mark, which means that it's managed to double its sales over the last few days. I'm sure Unfrozen and Hooded Horse will be very pleased indeed with those numbers.

As Hooded Horse and Unfrozen say in their announcement post, Olden Era has also reached a new 24-hour player peak of 60,000 on Steam. It's also available on PC Game Pass, but the studios haven't shared player numbers for Microsoft's proprietary platform.

Since launching last week, Heroes of Might and Magic: Olden Era has managed to amass over 2,400 reviews on Steam, and it's garnered a Very Positive rating, so it's obviously doing something right in the eyes of many of its players.

Hooded Horse and Unfrozen describe the new sales numbers as "beyond [their] wildest dreams" and pledge to continue "to work tirelessly to prove that [players'] faith in [them] is warranted", as well as to "ensure the legacy of this beloved series remains proud and alive".

If you're not familiar with Heroes of Might and Magic: Olden Era, it's the latest game in the Heroes of Might and Magic series, which is itself an offshoot of the wider Might and Magic franchise.

Heroes takes the series in a turn-based strategy direction, asking players to command armies of creatures and warriors in order to do battle in traditional fantasy-style settings.

If you'd like to see what all the fuss is about, you can check out Heroes of Might and Magic: Olden Era right now on PC via Steam Early Access, and it's also available as part of Game Pass (assuming you're subscribed to the correct tier, of course).

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/heroes-of-might-and-magic-olden-era-sales-500k
